authors_affiliation: Durmus Umutcan Uguz, Felix Berief, Steffen Leonhardt, Christoph Hoog Antink; Medical Information Technology, Helmholtz-Institute for Biomedical Engineering, RWTH Aachen University, Aachen, Germany

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (full test set, rank 19 of 41); also report a challenge validation-phase score of 0.576 and a 10-fold training cross-validation mean score of 0.57

self_reported_metrics.other: Full test set AUPRC 0.362. Per-test-database scores: Test Database 1: AUROC 0.967, F-measure 0.182, score 0.681; Test Database 2: AUROC 0.887, F-measure 0.412, score 0.556; Test Database 3: AUROC 0.806, F-measure 0.202, score -0.013. Official challenge validation-phase score: 0.576 (AUROC 0.906, AUPRC 0.478, accuracy 0.224, F-measure 0.413).

model.architecture_details: One-vs-Rest scheme with 25 independent XGBoost gradient-boosted-tree binary classifiers (one per scored class), each classifying a hand-engineered feature vector per recording; positive samples for class k reweighted by wk=(Ntot-Nk)/Nk to counter class imbalance; per-class decision thresholds tk tuned via cross-validation to optimize the challenge score.
model.key_novelty: Introduces a novel beat-to-beat Pearson-correlation analysis on Kors-transformed orthogonal XYZ leads to identify 'dissonant' (least similar) vs. 'accordant' (most similar) heartbeats relative to the median beat, isolating arrhythmic beats occurring among otherwise normal cycles; combined with classical fiducial-point timing/waveform features and wavelet-transform features, fed into per-class XGBoost classifiers.

input.leads_used: 12 (plus derived orthogonal XYZ leads via Kors transformation)
input.signal_length_window: not reported (whole-recording, beat-level feature extraction rather than a fixed window)
input.preprocessing: Low-pass filtered at 80Hz; baseline wander removed via a two-step median filter (200ms and 600ms window lengths); Kors transformation applied to derive orthogonal XYZ leads; beats segmented using BioSPPy and the Pan-Tompkins QRS detection algorithm

data.training_datasets: All official Challenge training datasets (CPSC, CPSC-unused, Georgia, PTB, PTB-XL, StPetersburg); generalization also evaluated via leave-one-dataset-out cross-validation
data.class_imbalance_handling: One-vs-Rest positive-sample reweighting per class by wk=(Ntot-Nk)/Nk (negative samples weighted 1); per-class decision thresholds tuned during cross-validation to optimize the challenge score

training.loss_function: not reported (XGBoost's internal gradient-boosting objective with per-class sample reweighting)
training.optimizer: not reported (XGBoost gradient boosting; hyperparameters gamma, min_child_weight, and max_depth were extensively tuned)
training.cross_validation_strategy: 10-fold cross-validation on the combined training data; additional leave-one-dataset-out cross-validation (holding out entire data sources) to evaluate generalization to unseen data sources
training.hardware: not reported (evaluation-system runtime on the official challenge system: 101 hours 28 minutes)

contribution.summary: Combines domain-specific ECG timing/waveform features with domain-agnostic wavelet features and a novel beat-to-beat Pearson-correlation analysis (on Kors-transformed XYZ leads) to detect atypical beats, classified via per-class One-vs-Rest XGBoost gradient-boosted trees with imbalance reweighting and tuned decision thresholds.
